summary: Introduces the Interior Observer framework: the observable universe lives inside a Schwarzschild black hole and the CMB is interior Hawking radiation. The paper derives zero-free-parameter predictions for the CMB temperature, dark-energy density, MOND acceleration scale, and Hubble rate, and compares the resulting expansion history with DESI DR1 BAO data.
full_title: Paper 1 — A Black Hole Cosmology with the CMB as Event Horizon Radiation
title_tag: Paper 1 — A Black Hole Cosmology | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Introduces the Interior Observer framework: the observable universe lives inside a Schwarzschild black hole and the CMB is interior Hawking radiation.

summary: Shows that semiclassical QFT on stationary Schwarzschild spacetime cannot generate the boost factor γ, then derives γ from the Carlip-Virasoro horizon algebra in the dimensionally reduced interior-observer setting. It also builds a holographic thermalization bridge from 1+1D horizon states to the observed 3+1D photon bath and characterizes the framework's space-time decoupling hierarchy.
short_form: Paper 2 — Deriving γ from Horizon Algebra
full_title: Paper 2 — Derivation of γ from Quantum Gravity, the Bogoliubov No-Go Theorem, the Holographic Thermalization Bridge, the Space-Time Decoupling Problem, and the Holographic Timescale Hierarchy
title_tag: Paper 2 — Deriving γ from Horizon Algebra | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Semiclassical QFT on Schwarzschild cannot supply the boost factor gamma; Paper 2 derives gamma instead from the Carlip-Virasoro horizon algebra.

summary: Derives the two-phase Vaidya-to-Oppenheimer-Snyder interior structure from Israel junction conditions with zero free parameters, including the observer coordinate and the total interior lifecycle. The paper also identifies the cosmological invariant T_CMB × R_U and proves the IO transfer function is analytic across the interior history.
full_title: Paper 3 — The Two-Phase Interior, the Cosmological Invariant, and the Observer Coordinate
title_tag: Paper 3 — The Two-Phase Interior Model | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Israel junction conditions derive the two-phase Vaidya-to-Oppenheimer-Snyder interior with zero free parameters, including the observer clock and crossing map.

summary: Collects the framework's first broad evidence packet by tying four horizon-interior observables to zero-fit predictions: the CMB temperature, dark-energy density, MOND acceleration scale, and boost factor γ. It also confronts the model with galaxy-formation timing and early JWST tensions, emphasizing the extra formation time available on the IO expansion history.
full_title: Paper 4 — Evidence for a Black Hole Interior: Four Horizon Connections, JWST Predictions, and Quantitative Tensions
title_tag: Paper 4 — Four Horizon Connections Tested | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Four horizon-interior observables — CMB temperature, dark energy, age, and angular scale — are tied to zero-parameter IO predictions in the first evidence packet.

summary: Explores three early structural questions: how black-hole spin affects the temperature prediction, whether the Vaidya radiation phase is compatible with an isotropic CMB bath, and what early-time interior physics is required. The analysis favors the Schwarzschild limit, rules out the naive Vaidya null-dust phase for CMB acoustics, and sharpens the framework's early-radiation picture.
short_form: Paper 5 — Kerr Limit and Radiation Phase
full_title: Paper 5 — The Kerr Limit, the Radiation Phase, and Early-Time Physics in Black Hole Cosmology
title_tag: Paper 5 — Kerr Limit and Radiation Phase | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 5 tests spin dependence, Vaidya compatibility, and early-time completeness, sharpening which early IO structures survive as viable theorem routes.

summary: Computes the full CMB TT spectrum with the CLASS Boltzmann code using the IO background geometry together with borrowed ΛCDM primordial inputs. The result is a clean geometric rescaling of the ΛCDM spectrum, with the acoustic peak structure preserved but shifted uniformly to larger angular scales, making the perturbation-sector gap explicit.
full_title: Paper 6 — The Full CMB Power Spectrum in Black Hole Cosmology: A CLASS Boltzmann Code Analysis
title_tag: Paper 6 — Full CMB Power Spectrum | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Computes the full CMB TT spectrum with the CLASS Boltzmann code using the IO background geometry together with borrowed ΛCDM primordial inputs.

summary: Applies the early IO cosmology to Big Bang nucleosynthesis and finds a mixed result: helium is viable, lithium is improved relative to ΛCDM, but deuterium is overproduced because of the low baryon-to-photon ratio. The paper also frames the resulting combined tension and introduces the c³ hypothesis as part of the early-stage response.
short_form: Paper 7 — BBN and the c³ Hypothesis
full_title: Paper 7 — Big Bang Nucleosynthesis, the Unified Tension, and the c³ Hypothesis
title_tag: Paper 7 — BBN and the c³ Hypothesis | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Early IO Big Bang nucleosynthesis gives viable helium, improved lithium, and a clear deuterium miss, establishing the first honest BBN boundary of the framework.

summary: Introduces the Rosetta Stone identity Δ = x⁴(1+γ²), which unifies the temporal decoupling, dark-energy normalization gap, and age tension into a single geometric relationship. The identity is numerically verified at high precision and becomes a central organizing formula for later papers.
full_title: Paper 8 — The Rosetta Stone Identity: Unifying Dark Energy, Temporal Decoupling, and Quantum Gravity in Black Hole Cosmology
title_tag: Paper 8 — The Rosetta Stone Identity | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: The Rosetta Stone identity Delta = x^4(1+gamma^2) unifies temporal decoupling, dark-energy normalization, and age tension into one exact relation.

summary: Closes the missing factor in the dark-energy derivation by identifying √(1+γ²) with the spectral-norm ratio of the Ashtekar-Barbero connection to the spin connection on the Schwarzschild horizon. The paper shows that the relevant connection pieces occupy orthogonal subspaces and that their spectral norms match exactly on the horizon slice used by the framework.
short_form: Paper 9 — Gauge Projection for Dark Energy
full_title: Paper 9 — The Gauge Projection: Completing the Dark Energy Derivation via the Spectral Norm of the Ashtekar-Barbero Connection
title_tag: Paper 9 — Gauge Projection for Dark Energy | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Gauge projection closes the dark-energy derivation by identifying the missing sqrt(1+gamma^2) factor with a connection-to-spin spectral norm ratio.

summary: Derives the complete IO Friedmann equation, combining the matter and bare-Λ sector with the energy projection √Δ and the curvature projection Δ^(-1/x²). The paper presents the framework's first full holographic background equation with zero free parameters and makes the modular-energy role of the projection factors explicit.
full_title: Paper 10 — The Complete Holographic Friedmann Equation — Zero Free Parameters, One Physical Principle
title_tag: Paper 10 — Complete Holographic Friedmann Equation | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: A zero-parameter cosmological constant derivation from the holographic Friedmann equation predicts Ω_Λ = 0.697, +0.92σ from the Planck 2018 reference.

summary: Subjects the framework to an extended adversarial stress test against CMB perturbations, BAO absolute distances, and BBN. It derives the Schur-complement curvature formula, identifies a species-dependent baryon correction, and introduces a modular-projection idea that substantially improves the framework's observational viability relative to its earlier form.
full_title: Paper 11 — Stress-Testing the Framework — Perturbation Theory, Curvature Decoherence, and the Species-Dependent Modular Projection
title_tag: Paper 11 — Stress-Testing the IO Framework | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Subjects the framework to an extended adversarial stress test against CMB perturbations, BAO absolute distances, and BBN.

summary: Responds to an internal kill-shot audit by replacing the ad hoc baryon fraction with the Baryon Dictionary Principle, which identifies baryons as the gauge-coupled subset of Oppenheimer-Snyder dust and fixes f_b = 2γ/x. The paper also introduces the discrete α-ladder and argues that α = 1 is the unique projection scaling consistent with the observed deuterium abundance.
full_title: Paper 12 — How the Kill Shots Die — The Baryon Dictionary Principle and the Rational Correction
title_tag: Paper 12 — The Baryon Dictionary Principle | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: The Baryon Dictionary Principle replaces the ad hoc baryon fraction with a theorem-grade inventory map for the gauge-coupled dust subset.

summary: Resolves the framework's large CMB temperature discrepancy by introducing the Gauge Thermal Transfer Principle. This principle assigns a boundary-to-bulk transfer law to conformal thermal observables in radial logarithmic coordinate and is used to lift the IO temperature prediction into agreement with the observed CMB without adding new fit parameters.
full_title: Paper 13 — The Gauge Thermal Transfer Principle — Closing the 230σ T_CMB Gap with Zero New Parameters
title_tag: Paper 13 — Gauge Thermal Transfer Principle | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: The Gauge Thermal Transfer Principle derives the CMB temperature as 2.7253 K, just 0.33σ from FIRAS, closing the framework's 230σ raw temperature gap.

summary: Audits three major structural vulnerabilities: frame dependence of the spectral norm, the N_eff closure, and the H₀ projection chain. The paper sharpens the horizon-intrinsic interpretation of K_gauge, refines the transfer identification, and maps which parts of the framework survive rigorous adversarial checking.
full_title: Paper 14 — Kill Shot Audit — Frame Covariance, the N_eff Closure, and the H₀ Projection
title_tag: Paper 14 — The Kill Shot Audit | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Audits three major structural vulnerabilities: frame dependence of the spectral norm, the N_eff closure, and the H₀ projection chain.

summary: Derives the Rosetta identity structurally within the framework through three theorems: the Quaternionic Norm Theorem, the Half-Density Carrier Theorem, and a conformal-transgression step that supplies the x² factor. It also proves a boundary no-go theorem showing that the compactness ratio x cannot be reconstructed from local horizon data alone.
full_title: Paper 15 — The Rosetta Boundary Observable Theorem — Structural Derivation of Δ = x⁴(1+γ²) from Quaternionic Half-Density Carriers
title_tag: Paper 15 — Rosetta Boundary Observable Theorem | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Quaternionic norm, half-density carrier, and asymptotic gluing theorems derive the Rosetta identity structurally inside the framework.

summary: Resolves the final degree question in the dark-energy derivation: why the gauge projection appears as √(1+γ²) rather than 1+γ². The paper develops a set of transfer-homogeneity and locality theorems, adds a composite-uniqueness argument, and narrows the allowed projection degree on the physical channel.
full_title: Paper 16 — The Gauge Projection Degree: Completing the Dark Energy Derivation via Transfer Homogeneity, Composite Uniqueness, and Geometric Slot Locality
title_tag: Paper 16 — The Gauge Projection Degree | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Resolves the final degree question in the dark-energy derivation: why the gauge projection appears as √(1+γ²) rather than 1+γ².

summary: Upgrades the Gauge Thermal Transfer Principle to theorem status inside a shared thermal-plus-gauge Hilbert space. The paper constructs the A-vacuum GNS state, proves its modular properties, and derives the Modular Projection Theorem as an operator-level closure of the earlier transfer principle.
full_title: Paper 17 — The Modular Projection Theorem: Operator-Level Closure of the Gauge Thermal Transfer Principle via Shared Hilbert Space Construction and Fiberwise KMS Rigidity
title_tag: Paper 17 — The Modular Projection Theorem | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: A shared thermal-plus-gauge Hilbert space upgrades the Gauge Thermal Transfer Principle to theorem status and supports the 2.7253 K CMB readout.

summary: Completes the operator-level closure program by upgrading both the Conformal Modular Principle and the Baryon Dictionary Principle to theorem-level status within the reduced observer algebra. It realizes the geometric modular term as a relative modular operator and derives the baryon fraction and associated projection structure on the same operator-theoretic footing.
full_title: Paper 18 — Modular Completion: Operator-Level Closure of the Conformal Modular Principle and the Baryon Dictionary Principle via Relative Modular Operators and Gauge-Sector Projection
title_tag: Paper 18 — Modular Completion and Baryons | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Operator closure upgrades both the Conformal Modular Principle and the Baryon Dictionary Principle to theorem-grade results inside one shared stack.

summary: Diagnoses the framework's P(k) catastrophe, derives the Baryon Scalarization Theorem, and builds the conditional homogeneous-background IO Friedmann equation in boundary-Hamiltonian form. It also proves a no-go against a universal projector and clarifies how observable-class Jacobians reshape baryon quantities across different sectors.
full_title: Paper 19 — Baryon Scalarization, the Universal Projector No-Go, the Boundary-Hamiltonian IO Friedmann Equation, and the Foundation Closure Audit
title_tag: Paper 19 — Baryon Scalarization and H₀ | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 19 derives baryon scalarization and the boundary-Hamiltonian IO Friedmann equation, kills the universal projector, and opens the Hubble-tension route by class.

summary: Addresses three foundation issues at once: the acoustic projection law, the radiation scope boundary, and the assembly gap in the background equation. It proves the Acoustic History Reduction and Acoustic Phase-Calibration theorems, predicts the CMB acoustic scale with no fitted parameters, and undertakes a deep audit of the BBN radiation sector.
full_title: Paper 20 — The Acoustic Projection Theorem, the Radiation Scope Boundary, the Bare Background Reconstruction, the Assembly Gap Diagnostic, and the BBN Radiation Sector Investigation
title_tag: Paper 20 — The Acoustic Projection Theorem | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Addresses three foundation issues at once: the acoustic projection law, the radiation scope boundary, and the assembly gap in the background equation.

summary: Derives AC1 from the existing Paper 15–20 stack and upgrades the key acoustic theorem from Paper 20 to derived status within the reduced scalar acoustic sector. It also isolates the SU(2) Geometric Weight as a candidate explanation for the remaining radiation-response deficit in deuterium.
full_title: Paper 21 — The AC1 Derivation, the SU(2) Geometric Weight, and the Radiation Response Problem
title_tag: Paper 21 — The AC1 Derivation | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 21 derives AC1 from the Papers 15-20 stack and upgrades the key acoustic theorem to derived status inside the reduced scalar acoustic sector.

summary: Constructs the full scalar/vector/tensor spatial channel infrastructure on the S³ interior sections and develops a wide no-go landscape that eliminates fluid-injection and uniform-dressing fixes for the radiation-response problem. The paper leaves a surviving rate-dressing bridge in which the boundary puncture algebra modifies bulk interaction cross-sections through the spatial Hodge complex.
full_title: Paper 22 — The Spatial Hodge Complex and the Rate-Dressing Bridge: Channel Infrastructure, No-Go Landscape, and Framework-Constant Candidate BBN Amplitudes
title_tag: Paper 22 — Spatial Hodge Rate Bridge | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 22 builds the full scalar/vector/tensor channel infrastructure on S^3 and kills broad no-go classes while isolating surviving bridge routes.

summary: Builds the primordial perturbation theory program on the Oppenheimer-Snyder interior, deriving the closed-FRW perturbation equations and constructing explicit boundary-to-bulk bridge operators. It also proves the No-Doubling Theorem and derives the white baseline n_s = 1 from isotropic puncture positions on the horizon.
short_form: Paper 23 — Primordial Perturbation Theory on OS
full_title: Paper 23 — Primordial Perturbation Theory on the Oppenheimer-Snyder Interior — The Bridge Operator, the No-Doubling Theorem, and the Spectral Index
title_tag: Paper 23 — Primordial Perturbation Theory on OS | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Primordial perturbation theory on the Oppenheimer-Snyder interior derives closed-FRW perturbation equations and explicit scalar bridge operators.

summary: Conditionally resolves the cosmological lithium problem through channel-resolved BBN rate dressing. The paper shows that the spin-2 TT metric perturbation couples only to nuclear quadrupole moments, shielding spherical nuclei while selectively suppressing beryllium-7 production through a quadrupole-sensitive mass-7 dressing channel.
full_title: Paper 24 — The Lithium Problem Solved - Channel-Resolved BBN, Quadrupole Isolation, and the Mass-7 TT Dressing
title_tag: Paper 24 — The Lithium Problem Solved | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Channel-resolved Big Bang Nucleosynthesis and quadrupole isolation resolve the cosmological lithium problem at Li-7/H = 1.75×10^-10, +0.55σ, with χ² = 1.13.

summary: Resolves the V-vs-V' class-membership problem for the weak-sector BBN dressing coefficient. Conditional on three physical identification premises, it proves V = K_gauge exactly via the Quadratic Thermal Covariance Theorem, corrects the weak amplitude to ε_w = K_gauge × L_1, and reports PRyMordial-verified BBN agreement with χ² = 1.
full_title: Paper 25 — The Weak-Sector Identity Pin — Quadratic Thermal Covariance, the Channel-Budget Equation, and the V-vs-V' Class-Membership Theorem
title_tag: Paper 25 — The Weak-Sector Identity Pin | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Quadratic Thermal Covariance and the channel-budget equation fix V = K_gauge on the weak sector, repairing the helium-side BBN dressing coefficient.

summary: Proposes IO-native replacements for ΛCDM-borrowed inputs in the framework's confrontation with Planck 2018 CMB data. The headline result is a conditional Hawking-boundary prediction for the primordial scalar amplitude, A_s = 2.007 × 10⁻⁹, and the paper also shows that clustering-sector baryon quantities do not belong in CMB perturbation slots.
full_title: Paper 26 — The Primordial Scalar Amplitude from the Hawking Boundary State, Toward IO-Native Replacements for ΛCDM-Borrowed Inputs, Source-Side Reduction, the CMB Baryon Class Diagnostic, and the Effective Optical Damping Parameter
title_tag: Paper 26 — The Primordial Scalar Amplitude | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 26 replaces borrowed LambdaCDM confrontation inputs with IO-native CMB ingredients, opening the native route to Planck 2018 TT comparison.

summary: Presents the first fully IO-native CLASS Boltzmann-code confrontation with Planck 2018 TT data and diagnoses the residual mismatch as an architectural conflict between the IO boundary-projective multi-baryon framework and CLASS's single-baryon bulk simulator. The paper promotes multiple theorem candidates, corrects source-side perturbation ingredients, and audits the full scalar-amplitude normalization chain.
full_title: Paper 27 — Source-Side Perturbation Sector Closure, the First Fully IO-Native Planck Confrontation, and the Conditional Elevation Program
title_tag: Paper 27 — Source-Side Perturbation Sector Closure | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: The first fully IO-native CLASS confrontation with Planck 2018 TT diagnoses the remaining mismatch as an architectural issue rather than a fit problem.

summary: Builds the main theorem scaffold for the primordial scalar power spectrum and derives the conditional zero-fit prediction n_s = 0.9639. Key ingredients include the Hawking Accumulation Theorem, the DtN Boundary Hessian identification on the Painlevé-Gullstrand collar, and the Boundary Fixed-Point Principle, along with a proper-time formation-clock theorem that eases the JWST "impossible galaxy" tension.
full_title: Paper 28 — The Primordial Spectral Index, the JWST Impossible Galaxy Tension Eased, IO-Native Scale Invariance, the DtN Boundary Hessian, and the Boundary Fixed-Point Principle
title_tag: Paper 28 — The Primordial Spectral Index | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: The primordial spectral index is derived as n_s = 0.9639, only 0.24σ from Planck 2018, using the DtN boundary Hessian and the Boundary Fixed-Point Principle.

summary: Rebuilds the confrontation layer on the active Paper 10 legacy branch, retires the mixed-branch package, and closes the branch self-consistency audit. On the active legacy package, cosmic chronometers give χ² = 14.70 and the derived/scoped DESI DR2 GCcomb galaxy/quasar BAO route gives χ² = 27.74, for combined χ² = 42.44 versus 46.40 for the fixed-parameter Planck ΛCDM reference.
full_title: Paper 29 — The Branch Consistency Audit: Resolving the Assembly Gap, the Schur Definitive Package, and Direct Expansion-Rate Confrontation
title_tag: Paper 29 — The Branch Consistency Audit | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Rebuilds the confrontation layer on the active Paper 10 legacy branch, retires the mixed-branch package, and closes the branch self-consistency audit.

summary: Recomputes the twenty-test confrontation on the active Paper 10 legacy branch. Pantheon+ and TDCOSMO still favor IO over the fixed-parameter Planck reference, the Alcock-Paczyński test still favors Planck, and several mixed-branch headline numbers change materially on the rebuilt branch, especially in the late-structure sector.
full_title: Paper 30 — One Mass, Zero Fits: Twenty Independent Tests of a Universe Inside a Black Hole
title_tag: Paper 30 — One Mass, Zero Fits | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 30 rebuilds the twenty-test confrontation on the active Paper 10 legacy branch, replacing the mixed-branch package with one runtime surface.

summary: Recomputes the Weyl-response and late-time observable map on the active Paper 10 legacy branch. The current branch gives Σ_IO = 0.8114, S₈,weyl = 0.8339, α_Φ ≈ 2.005 in the E_G pipeline, and a derived/scoped galaxy/quasar BAO closure on the current DESI DR2 GCcomb definition.
full_title: Paper 31 — Gravitational Slip from Schwarzschild Interior Geometry: η = 0.623, the S₈ Tension, and a Scoped Audit of the IO Framework
title_tag: Paper 31 — Gravitational Slip from Geometry | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 31 recomputes the Weyl-response and late-time observable map on the active Paper 10 legacy branch, including the geometric pre-drag ruler.

summary: Paper 32 closes the six remaining open problems on their active scoped sectors, retires all four semiclassical principles on their stated scopes, and carries the late-time chain through x_crit, hidden-support recollapse, and the Paper 32 closure/lifecycle program. This card reflects the current Zenodo title/description while the site’s branch-sensitive numerics follow the active Paper 10 legacy reconstruction.
short_form: Paper 32 — Closure: The Complete IO Framework
full_title: Paper 32 — Closure: The Complete Interior Observer Framework — 42: The Answer Was Simple, The Question Was "Where Am I?"
title_tag: Paper 32 — Closure: The Complete IO Framework | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 32 closes six remaining open problems on their active scoped sectors, retires four semiclassical principles, and carries the framework to x_crit.

summary: An event horizon speaks three quantum languages to the classical interior it contains — and those three languages are sufficient to derive every major cosmological observable with zero fitted parameters. The Interior Observer (IO) framework models the observable universe as the interior of a Schwarzschild black hole. Across 32 papers, it derives the CMB temperature, dark energy density, baryon fraction, three BBN abundances (χ² = 1.13), the spectral index, the scalar amplitude, and the gravitational slip — all from one mass and one external constant. This paper reveals the organizing principle: the boundary-to-bulk crossings fall into three channels. Channel 1 (Geometric) is always on — the boundary's gauge algebra permanently imprints on bulk geometry. Channel 2 (Thermal) is state-gated — processes in thermal equilibrium with the Hawking/KMS boundary state receive geometric dressing. Channel 3 (Fermionic) is density-gated — at Planck density, boundary spinor data sources the bulk spin fluid that drives the torsion bounce, with R_eq/R_bounce = 1.00016 from representation theory alone. The framework closes a logical loop: Paper 1 assumed the bounce; Paper 32 derives it from the boundary's own quantum data. The premises generate the mathematics that validates the premises. No other cosmological framework derives its complete lifecycle — birth, expansion, recollapse, and rebirth — with zero continuously fitted parameters. Includes: a geometric dark-matter alternative (predicting continued null results from direct detection), a conditional no-go for quantum gravity signatures in the muon g−2, and the Holographic Selection Rule governing which observables see the boundary and which do not. Interactive bridge map: https://dfife.github.io/bridge-map.html
short_form: Paper 33 — Three Languages of the Horizon
full_title: Paper 33 — The Quantum Gravity Bridge: Conditional Holographic Unification of General Relativity and Quantum Mechanics with 32 Zero-Parameter Derivations from Inside a Schwarzschild Horizon
title_tag: Paper 33 — Three Languages of the Horizon | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 33 reframes the archive as three horizon languages and packages 32 zero-parameter derivations linking general relativity, quantum mechanics, and cosmology.

summary: The Hubble tension — the 5σ discrepancy between the Planck CMB measurement (67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc) and the SH0ES Cepheid distance ladder (73.0 ± 1.0 km/s/Mpc) — is resolved with zero fitted parameters. The resolution follows from one recognition: different H₀ measurement methods belong to different observable classes on the IO α-ladder, and each class sees a different effective expansion rate dressed by the boundary transit factor f_Γ = 1/(1+γ²). The inverse-projector law H_eff(α) = H₀ × f_Γ^(1−α) is derived for the late-time homogeneous estimator family, and two new bridge extensions complete the classification for SH0ES, TRGB, and TDCOSMO. Six methods fall within 0.57σ of prediction, with SH0ES at +0.04σ.
full_title: Paper 34 — The Hubble Tension Resolved: Who Is Right About H₀? Everyone.
title_tag: Paper 34 — The Hubble Tension Resolved | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: A zero-parameter Hubble tension resolution puts six H₀ methods within 0.57σ of prediction, with SH0ES at +0.04σ, by classifying observables on the IO α-ladder.

summary: Paper 35 packages four confrontation layers on top of the active Paper 10 legacy branch. It closes the late-time baryon-to-photon ratio route, sharpens the BBN bulk-frame η route, reduces baryogenesis to an exact topological obstruction on the current stack, shows that the IO master clock gives 46-48% more formation time than Planck ΛCDM at z > 10, corrects the observer-side dark-energy mapping away from the retired w = -1/3 carryover, and frames continuing direct-detection null results as the expected outcome of the geometric dark-sector reading.
full_title: Paper 35 — Four Problems, One Horizon Baryogenesis, Impossible Galaxies, Dark Energy, and the Dark Matter Null from Inside a Schwarzschild Horizon
title_tag: Paper 35 — Four Problems, One Horizon | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 35 closes late-time eta_IO, gives 46-48% more formation time than Planck LambdaCDM at z > 10, and reframes dark-energy and dark-matter nulls geometrically.
